activities.instructions: Choose the correct reporting verb form.
activities.prompt: Maya ___ that the office was closed.
activities.explanation: Sin un objeto personal usamos «said that».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct reporting verb form.
activities.prompt: Maya ___ me that the office was closed.
activities.explanation: «Tell» necesita la persona directamente: «told me».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct backshifted verb.
activities.prompt: “I work remotely,” Leo said. Leo said that he ___ remotely.
activities.explanation: Con «said», el presente simple retrocede a pasado simple.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct backshifted form.
activities.prompt: “I am waiting outside,” Nora said. Nora said that she ___ outside.
activities.explanation: El presente continuo retrocede a pasado continuo.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct pronoun.
activities.prompt: Lena said, “I am tired.” Lena said that ___ was tired.
activities.explanation: El «I» original se refiere a Lena y pasa a «she».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct past perfect form.
activities.prompt: “I lost the receipt,” Amir said. Amir said that he ___ the receipt.
activities.explanation: El pasado simple retrocede a past perfect: «had lost».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct backshifted present perfect.
activities.prompt: “We have completed the survey,” they said. They said that they ___ the survey.
activities.explanation: Present perfect pasa a past perfect.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct change from “will”.
activities.prompt: “I will call the supplier,” Eva said. Eva said that she ___ call the supplier.
activities.explanation: «Will» retrocede a «would».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct change from “can”.
activities.prompt: “I can repair the lock,” Ravi said. Ravi said that he ___ repair the lock.
activities.explanation: «Can» retrocede a «could».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct listener pronoun.
activities.prompt: Mina told Leo, “I can help you.” Mina told Leo that she could help ___.
activities.explanation: El «you» original se refiere a Leo y pasa a «him».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct time reference when reporting two days later.
activities.prompt: On Monday Sara said, “I will finish today.” On Wednesday we reported that Sara would finish ___.
activities.explanation: El «today» del lunes pasa a «that day».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct future reference when reporting later.
activities.prompt: “The parcel will arrive tomorrow,” Jo said. Later, we reported that it would arrive ___.
activities.explanation: «Tomorrow» cambia a «the next day».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct past time reference when reporting later.
activities.prompt: “I met her yesterday,” Ben said. Ben said that he had met her ___.
activities.explanation: «Yesterday» cambia a «the day before».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct change of place and demonstrative.
activities.prompt: After leaving the site, Ana reported “I keep these tools here” as: Ana said that she kept ___ tools ___.
activities.explanation: Al cambiar lugar y perspectiva, «these» pasa a «those» y «here» a «there».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct form for an obligation reported later.
activities.prompt: “I must renew my permit,” Salma said. Salma said that she ___ renew her permit.
activities.explanation: El «must» de obligación cambia normalmente a «had to».

activities.instructions: Choose the sentence that reports both pronouns correctly.
activities.prompt: Nora told Amir, “I left your folder on my desk.”

options.text: Nora told Amir that she had left his folder on her desk.
Nora told Amir that I had left your folder on my desk.
Nora said Amir that she left her folder on his desk.
Nora told to Amir that he had left her folder.

activities.explanation: «I/my» se refieren a Nora y «your» a Amir.

activities.instructions: Choose the complete report made several days later.
activities.prompt: On Friday Leo said, “I will bring this sample here tomorrow.”

options.text: Leo said that he would bring that sample there the next day.
Leo said that I will bring this sample here tomorrow.
Leo told that he would brought those sample yesterday.
Leo said me he will bring that sample there.

activities.explanation: Cambian «I», «will», «this», «here» y «tomorrow».

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of a still-true scientific fact.
activities.prompt: The teacher said, “Water freezes at 0°C.”

options.text: The teacher said that water freezes at 0°C.
The teacher told that water froze at 0°C.
The teacher said water had frozen always at 0°C.
The teacher told us water will freeze at 0°C yesterday.

activities.explanation: Un hecho general vigente puede mantener el presente.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report with a present reporting verb.
activities.prompt: Every morning Mina says, “I need the sales figures by noon.”

options.text: Mina says that she needs the sales figures by noon.
Mina says that she needed the sales figures the day before.
Mina told that she need the figures.
Mina says me she had needed them.

activities.explanation: Con «says» en presente no hacemos backshift.

activities.instructions: Choose the correct report of a negative statement.
activities.prompt: “We haven't received the replacement parts,” the engineers said.

options.text: The engineers said that they hadn't received the replacement parts.
The engineers told that they haven't receive the parts.
The engineers said they didn't received the parts.
The engineers told to us that we hadn't received them.

activities.explanation: Present perfect negativo pasa a past perfect negativo.

activities.instructions: Choose the only fully accurate report made the following week.
activities.prompt: On Monday Priya told Omar, “I can't meet you here tomorrow because I have lost my travel card.”

options.text: Priya told Omar that she couldn't meet him there the next day because she had lost her travel card.
Priya said Omar that she can't meet you here tomorrow because she lost my card.
Priya told to Omar she couldn't met him there because she has lost her card.
Priya told Omar that he couldn't meet her the day before because he had lost his card.

activities.explanation: La opción correcta adapta modal, pronombres, lugar, tiempo y present perfect.

activities.instructions: Choose the only fully accurate report made later.
activities.prompt: Rosa said, “I was checking these figures when my computer stopped.”

options.text: Rosa said that she had been checking those figures when her computer stopped.
Rosa told that I was checking these figures when my computer stops.
Rosa said she is checking those figures when her computer had stop.
Rosa said to she that she had checked this figures now.

activities.explanation: El pasado continuo retrocede a past perfect continuous; el evento puntual con «when» permanece claro en pasado simple.

activities.instructions: Choose the version that correctly distinguishes “said” and “told”.
activities.prompt: Which pair uses “say” and “tell” with their listeners correctly?

options.text: Elena said to me that she had already left, and I told Sam that she was unavailable.
Elena said me that she had left, and I told that Sam she was unavailable.
Elena told to me she left, and I said Sam that she unavailable.
Elena said to that she had left, and I told to Sam she was unavailable.

activities.explanation: «Said to me» y «told Sam» usan correctamente sus objetos.

activities.instructions: Choose the accurate report with obligation and time reference.
activities.prompt: On 3 May, Amina said, “I must submit this form next week.”

options.text: Amina said that she had to submit that form the following week.
Amina said she musted submit this form last week.
Amina told that she has to submitted those form next week.
Amina said me that I had to submit that form tomorrow.

activities.explanation: Obligación «must» pasa a «had to» y cambian demostrativo y referencia semanal.

activities.instructions: Choose the report that keeps unchanged forms appropriately.
activities.prompt: Marta said, “I had already eaten, so I couldn't join the lunch.”

options.text: Marta said that she had already eaten, so she couldn't join the lunch.
Marta said that she has already ate, so she can't joined the lunch.
Marta told that I had eaten, so I couldn't join lunch.
Marta said me she had been eat, so she wouldn't joining.

activities.explanation: Past perfect y «couldn't» no necesitan retroceder más; solo cambian los pronombres.
